# Software bill of materials

Every release ships a CycloneDX-JSON SBOM per archive.

## What's in it

A machine-readable inventory:

- Each Go module in `go.sum` with version + hash.
- The shellboto binary itself with its ldflags'd version.
- Transitive deps.
- Component classifications (library, application).

Useful for:

- Compliance regimes that require SBOM provenance.
- Vulnerability scanners (Trivy, Grype) that ingest CycloneDX.
- "What version of `creack/pty` is in shellboto v0.2.0?" without
  cloning source.

## Generated by syft

goreleaser configures:

```yaml
sboms:
  - artifacts: archive
    cmd: syft
    args: ["$artifact", "--output", "cyclonedx-json=$document"]
```

One SBOM per archive. Artifact names:

```
shellboto_0.2.0_linux_x86_64.tar.gz.sbom.json
shellboto_0.2.0_linux_arm64.tar.gz.sbom.json
shellboto_0.2.0_darwin_x86_64.tar.gz.sbom.json
shellboto_0.2.0_darwin_arm64.tar.gz.sbom.json
```

Attached to the GitHub Release like any other artifact.

## Installing syft

`scripts/install-dev-tools.sh` includes syft. Manually:

```bash
curl -sSfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anchore/syft/main/install.sh | sh -s -- -b /usr/local/bin
```

## Running locally

```bash
syft dist/shellboto_0.2.0_linux_x86_64.tar.gz --output cyclonedx-json
```

## Consuming SBOMs

### Vulnerability scan with Grype

```bash
grype sbom:shellboto_0.2.0_linux_x86_64.tar.gz.sbom.json
```

### Inventory query with jq

```bash
jq '.components[] | {name, version}' \
    shellboto_0.2.0_linux_x86_64.tar.gz.sbom.json
```

### Compliance attestation

SBOMs are self-signing-free — anyone can regenerate them from the
same archive. Combine with checksums.txt + GPG signature (future
work) for an attestable chain.

## Why CycloneDX and not SPDX

CycloneDX is the OWASP-backed standard and what the security
tooling ecosystem (Grype, Dependency-Track) supports most
natively. SPDX is valid too; syft supports both. We pick one to
keep the release small.

If your downstream pipeline wants SPDX:

```bash
syft dist/shellboto_0.2.0_linux_x86_64.tar.gz --output spdx-json
```

Run against the archive after download.
